race.name: Mt. Ashwabay Summit
race.tagline: Thirty kilometers of skiathlon racing on Wisconsin's Lake Superior shore — where the Bayfield Peninsula's Apostle Islands backdrop and mixed-technique format test versatility in the Northwoods.

climate.description: February on the Bayfield Peninsula benefits from Lake Superior's snow belt effect. Temperatures typically -16°C to -4°C. Heavy lake-effect snow ensures deep snowpack. The peninsula's exposed position can bring significant wind chill from the lake.
challenges: Lake Superior wind chill on exposed sections
Deep cold in February — frostbite risk
Lake-effect snow squalls can reduce visibility
Remote Bayfield Peninsula location requires planning

course.primary: Skiathlon format at Mt. Ashwabay on the Bayfield Peninsula — half classic, half skate with a transition zone. Rolling terrain through northern Wisconsin hardwood and conifer forest with views toward the Apostle Islands in Lake Superior. The Mt. Ashwabay trail system provides genuine terrain variation with climbs and technical descents.
course.grooming: Good grooming by dedicated local volunteers
features: Skiathlon format — classic and skate in one race
Mt. Ashwabay on the Bayfield Peninsula, Lake Superior shore
Views toward the Apostle Islands National Lakeshore
Rolling Northwoods terrain with genuine climbs and descents
Lake Superior snow belt ensures deep snowpack

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Skiathlon format is a distinctive feature — few US citizen races offer this. Exceptional snow reliability and Lake Superior/Apostle Islands scenery. Remote location and small field limit several scores. Good technical terrain on the Mt. Ashwabay trail system.

history.summary: The Mt. Ashwabay Summit is a skiathlon event on Wisconsin's Bayfield Peninsula, overlooking Lake Superior and the Apostle Islands. Mt. Ashwabay is a community-owned ski area that has served the Bayfield area since the 1960s. The skiathlon format — combining classic and skate technique in a single race — tests versatile Nordic skills in one of the snowiest areas of the upper Midwest.
notable_facts: Skiathlon format combines classic and skate technique in one race
Mt. Ashwabay is a community-owned ski area since the 1960s
Bayfield Peninsula overlooks the Apostle Islands National Lakeshore
The Apostle Islands are a US National Lakeshore with sea caves and historic lighthouses
Bayfield is known as the gateway to the Apostle Islands
Lake Superior's snow belt delivers over 100 inches of snow to the peninsula annually

videos.curation_reason: Cross-country skiing footage at Mt. Ashwabay providing course perspective and terrain overview.

videos.title: Mt. Ashwabay XC 1-3-11
videos.description: This is a time lapse of some of Mt. Ashwabay's XC trails on 1-3-11.  The route is Sugarbush Classic, Seagull Classic, Anchor, Roller Coaster.  Conditions are rated Fair-Poor by normal standards, although considering the weather we've had, the snow was in good shape.  The base is approx 4", and there are rocks and sticks poking through the bottom of the tracks.  The worst part is definitely Sugarbush near the trail head.  Once you get up the hill and into the valleys, the conditions improve greatly.  Definitely bring the rock skis, but get out and enjoy the snow we have!  The Alpine hill is in good condition for those looking for space to skate/train.

videos.title: Mt. Ashwabay Nordic: Seagull Hill
videos.description: Take a ride down the Seagull Hill.

videos.title: Summit Race 2013
videos.description:
Mt. Ashwabay Summit Ski Race February 2nd, 2013.

We have an 8 km classic race, 19 km pursuit race, a 11 km skate race, a 3.5 km youth race and a .5 km kids race (this is a non-timed event for those 8 and under).

For more information, and registration information, please visit http://www.ashwabaysummitskirace.com/

If you like this film you can support more like it at sunridgemovingpictures.com just put a donation in your shopping cart
